key_insight: HVAF spraying can produce thick NiTi coatings that retain martensitic transformation and shape-memory functionality, despite a heterogeneous as-sprayed microstructure.
engineering_relevance: This offers a practical route to add functional, self-actuating NiTi surfaces to steel components for wear, friction, or thermal-response applications.
method: HVAF spray-deposited NiTi onto mild steel using four process parameter sets.
Characterized composition, porosity, microstructure, and phase transformation behavior.
Assessed functional response with annealing, 3-point bending, nanoindentation, and scratch tests.
evidence_signal: The coatings were 100–300 µm thick and were shown to transform martensitically and exhibit thermal actuation and shape-memory effects after annealing.
watch_item: As-sprayed coatings contained voids, oxides, dislocations, and internal stress, so post-spray annealing and property consistency are important concerns.
paper_title: HVAF Spraying of NiTi Coatings: Microstructure, Phase Transformation and Shape Memory Behavior
